title: Irish Fish Chowder
description: A hearty and comforting Irish-inspired fish chowder, perfect for a chilly evening. This dish combines the freshness of fish with the richness of potatoes and cream.
instructions: Melt 1 tablespoon of Irish butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the diced potatoes and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the fish pieces and cook until opaque, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the flour and stir to combine, cooking for 1-2 minutes.
Gradually add the codfish stock, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Stir in the heavy cream and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until heated through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley or chives if desired.
